Idiom (Bono): Se wo werɛ fi na wosan kɔfa a, yenkyi.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Conditional subordinate clause followed by main clause; verb serialization (werɛ fi / wosan kɔfa).
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: It is not wrong to correct a mistake after realizing it.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Sɛ woyɛ mfomso na wokɔsesa no a, ɛnyɛ bɔne.
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, moral advice, education, family guidance.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Widely used Akan-Bono proverb emphasizing humility and corrective wisdom in traditional councils.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Bono): [sɛ wo wɛrɛ fi na wɔsan kɔfa a jɛŋkji]
First Example Sentence: Se wo werɛ fi na wosan kɔfa a, yenkyi, enti kɔsrɛ ne fakyɛ.
Second Example Sentence: Ɔpanyin no kae sɛ se wo werɛ fi na wosan kɔfa a, yenkyi.
Third Example Sentence: Yɛn abusua mu no, se wo werɛ fi na wosan kɔfa a, yenkyi yɛ mmara.

Idiom (Bono): Dua koro gye mframa a, ebu.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Independent declarative clause; metaphorical subject-object structure.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: One person alone is vulnerable without support.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Onipa baako nni ahoɔden a obetumi agyina ne ho.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Agrarian imagery referencing forest ecology in Bono regions.
First Example Sentence: Dua koro gye mframa a, ebu, enti mommra yɛnyɛ no bom.
Second Example Sentence: Ɔkyerɛkyerɛfo no de dua koro gye mframa a, ebu kyerɛɛ sukuufo no.
Third Example Sentence: Wopɛ sɛ wodi adwuma no nkutoo? Dua koro gye mframa a, ebu.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Simple negative factual clause; animal metaphor.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A person cannot produce what is outside their nature.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional ecological observation about species reproduction.
First Example Sentence: Ɔkɔtɔ nwo anoma, enti mma yɛnhwɛ nea ɛboro ne tumi.
Second Example Sentence: Ɔpanyin no kae sɛ ɔkɔtɔ nwo anoma bere a wɔregye ne di.
Third Example Sentence: Wopɛ sɛ ɔyɛ nea ɔnnim? Ɔkɔtɔ nwo anoma.

Idiom (Bono): Anomaa antu a, ɔbɔ ne tirim.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Sɛ wunnya mmɔden a wobehyia ɔhaw.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Bird imagery tied to farming seasons.
First Example Sentence: Kenkan na mmɔ mmɔden, na anomaa antu a, ɔbɔ ne tirim.
Second Example Sentence: Ɔma ne ba no kae sɛ anomaa antu a, ɔbɔ ne tirim.
Third Example Sentence: Wogyaa adwuma no? Anomaa antu a, ɔbɔ ne tirim.

Idiom (Bono): Nsuo bɔ dam a, ɛnsɔ fam.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Sɛ basabasa ba a, asomdwoe nni hɔ.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from river flooding patterns in Bono farmlands.
First Example Sentence: Bere a akasakasa no hyɛe ase no, nsuo bɔ dam a, ɛnsɔ fam.
Second Example Sentence: Ɔtwerɛfo no de nsuo bɔ dam a, ɛnsɔ fam kyerɛɛ ɔman no tebea.
Third Example Sentence: Nsuo bɔ dam a, ɛnsɔ fam, enti momma yɛnsiesie asɛm no.

Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Onipa a ɔwɔ ayamye yɛ ɔdefo paa.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in Bono communal value systems.
First Example Sentence: Ma wo koma nyɛ pa, efisɛ akomam pa yɛ sika.
Second Example Sentence: Ɔkae sɛ akomam pa yɛ sika sen sika kɔkɔɔ.
Third Example Sentence: Yɛn nananom kaa akomam pa yɛ sika bere tenten.

Idiom (Bono): Tiri nkɔ aguare a, akyidifo nni hɔ.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Without initiative, there are no followers.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Sɛ wunni akokoduru a, obi rento wo akyi.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Refers to traditional dance and communal leadership roles.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Bono): [tiri nkɔ aguare a akyidifo nni hɔ]
First Example Sentence: Wopɛ sɛ woyɛ ɔkannifo? Tiri nkɔ aguare a, akyidifo nni hɔ.
Second Example Sentence: Ɔkyerɛkyerɛfo no de tiri nkɔ aguare a, akyidifo nni hɔ hyɛɛ wɔn nkuran.
Third Example Sentence: Tiri nkɔ aguare a, akyidifo nni hɔ na adwuma no renkɔ yie.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Parallel clause structure; reciprocal teaching form.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: If someone does not know, another teaches.
First Example Sentence: Sɛ wunnim a, obi nnim a, obi kyere.
Second Example Sentence: Yɛn sukuu mu no, obi nnim a, obi kyere yɛ adwumadi.
Third Example Sentence: Ɔma ne ba no kae sɛ obi nnim a, obi kyere.

Idiom (Bono): Wo nsa ka adwuma a, fa wo koma ka ho.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Imperative clause with parallel verb phrase.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Craftsmanship ethic in Bono artisan traditions.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Bono): [wo nsa ka adwuma a fa wo koma ka ho]
First Example Sentence: Wo nsa ka adwuma a, fa wo koma ka ho na wobedi nkonim.
Second Example Sentence: Ɔpanyin no hyɛɛ wɔn nkuran sɛ wo nsa ka adwuma a, fa wo koma ka ho.
Third Example Sentence: Yɛn adwumakuo mu no, wo nsa ka adwuma a, fa wo koma ka ho yɛ mmara.

Idiom (Bono): Abofra bo nnua a, na ɔrehwehwɛ akyekyedeɛ.
Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Conditional clause with progressive verb form.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Sɛ abofra reyɛ basabasa a, ɛwɔ nea ɔpɛ.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rural imagery tied to children throwing stones at fruit trees.
Phonetic Transcription (IPA, Bono): [abofra bo nnua a na ɔrehwɛ akyekyedeɛ]
First Example Sentence: Ɔba no bo nnua, na abofra bo nnua a, na ɔrehwehwɛ akyekyedeɛ.
Second Example Sentence: Ɔkae no serew mu sɛ abofra bo nnua a, na ɔrehwehwɛ akyekyedeɛ.
Third Example Sentence: Bere a ɔhyɛɛ ase teeɛm no, mekae sɛ abofra bo nnua a, na ɔrehwehwɛ akyekyedeɛ.
